item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They might curse the aquarium if the service is really bad. They're just pretending to be regular eels, after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, they wouldn't do that. They're conflict-averse.

If the service sucks, they just won't come back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that is how an eel do

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eel gotta pay for the meal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the upvote! :3

I did a fair bit of trimming to try and keep this to a short read. I prefer shorter reads myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got the impression from the article that they were out dining, and now I just wonder where they get the money from and where they originate.

My idea is that they're actually ancient Persian eel deities, but they like to pretend they're just regular eels so they can be tourists.

I also can't help but wonder if, occasionally, they've left a mucus letter explaining their dissatisfaction with the service.

I hadn't thought of that! It's a great image. xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this - I got the impression from the article that they were out dining, and now I just wonder where they get the money from and where they originate.

I also can't help but wonder if, occasionally, they've left a mucus letter explaining their dissatisfaction with the service.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
one of these days I'm gonna put together a list of appropriate SCP links, to comprise the beginnings of an informal Cute Hub, heh

We could definitely use something like that, whenever someone is just in need of an "aww" moment in their day. You could probably make a thread in the Foundation Universe forum asking people for recommendations. We've got threads with people's favorite scary SCPs, favorite mythological SCPs, and so on.

ETA: also, thank you for the nice comment! :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahhh, I wish I could upvote this once each for every eel. xD

Relatedly, one of these days I'm gonna put together a list of appropriate SCP links, to comprise the beginnings of an informal Cute Hub, heh. Might as well start with this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is refreshing since it's short and cute. No extra logs or anything. Just adorable critters that give money for feeding them. Here's my upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found a silver coin in a puddle on my desk. I guess it's yours now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Godsdamnit, Zyn, you seem to have some kind of direct link to my Aww reflex! Your polite Persian gourmand eels can feast upon my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: they are good smol bois

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What very good boys

Yee! :D
Yee! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, these eels are just looking for a good meal at an exotic place with a nice atmosphere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was halfway expecting the translation to be "So long, and thanks for all the fish." Wrong marine animal, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i am glad you like the cute eels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First SCP that i read from Zyn since i joined the site. Seems good! cute lil eels

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
''Thanku!''

oh my god tiny eels tiny thankus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
they produce money when you feed them

Well, they're also there for the experience and the atmosphere!

No, but this was great. :D

:D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
European continents

North American and European. Two continents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Roget! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a clarification, European continents? I was taught there was only one of those, called Europe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First we had an eel whose most noticeable trait was its extreme length, and it produces amnestics when you feed it.

Now we have eels whose most noticeable trait is their extreme politeness, and they produce money when you feed them.

Coincidence??? >:V No, but this was great. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should have guessed Zyn wrote this. It's short and sweet and just the right amount of weird. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My favorite part is probably this:

It is my headcanon that their moving in and out of the burrows is accompanied by a “nyeeeeoooop” sound effect.

Imagining that with them saying: ''Thanku!'' is the best.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you like the tiny tourist eels!

My favorite part of this was probably trying to figure out how to write the containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're adorable! Beautiful in it's simplicity and the clever concept. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aweh, thanks! :D

i admittedly had a lot of fun looking at the pictures of the tiny eels on wikipedia

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot.

Its simple, its cute, its short, and those pictures are fantastic.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I imagine that if there was any way to reliably determine where the eels would visit next, the Foundation would probably experiment with providing gourmet aquatic food just to see if the eels would tip more.

Alternatively, offering a more romantic atmosphere for the eels that arrive in pairs?

kinda spoilers, I guess
hide

The eels probably aren't the most savvy of tourists anyway.

ETA: thank you for the +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's good that they're polite enough to try and tip when eating in groups, but it seems like they might be skimping a bit when a lot of them are together. Shame on those tiny eels.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to all these lovely people who read this over in chat!

  • jishwa for wording details
  • Modern_Erasmus for helpful linguistic rant
  • Wogglebug for thinking it was fun
  • Nuke_Fury for phrasing feex
  • AidenEldritch for being chill
  • Sirens for liking the containments

Image sources:

  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%E8%8A%B1%E5%9C%92%E9%B0%BB.JPG
    • Licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 Unported license
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Heteroconger_hassi_05.jpg
    • Licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 Unported license
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Red_Sea_Garden_Eel_-_Gorgasia_sillneri.jpg
    • Licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 2.0 Generic license
Spoilers?
hide

These eels are pretty old existence-wise, but they like modern dining and vacations. Tryin’ to be hip and stuff.

It is my headcanon that their moving in and out of the burrows is accompanied by a “nyeeeeoooop” sound effect.

Additional reading:
Additional reading and other fun stuff:

So… I really like aquariums. And I really like documentaries. And I also really like Sir David Attenborough!

There's a video of Sir David's documentary, "Desert Seas" on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cpkG1Vn81oU&t=3s at around the 24:27 mark, you can see garden eels going in and out of their burrows.

People also keep garden eels in personal aquariums! It's pretty hard to feed them though. http://www.advancedaquarist.com/2011/7/fish2

Things I referred to while writing:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indo-Pacific
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sea_Life_London_Aquarium
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aquarium_Berlin
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monterey_Bay_Aquarium
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heterocongrinae
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siglos
http://www.mypersiancorner.com/2013/06/how-can-i-thank-you-let-me-count-ways.html
http://www.wikihow.com/Say-Thank-You-in-Persian
